title: Calendar Cycles in the Quran - 19-Year Meton Miracle
description: The Quran's number 19 (74:30) connects to the 19-year Metonic cycle that synchronizes lunar and solar calendars. Full astronomical analysis.

faqs.question: What is the 19-year cycle in the Quran?
faqs.answer: Surah Al-Muddaththir 74:30 states 'over it are 19'. The number 19 is the length of the Metonic cycle - the period after which lunar and solar calendars realign (19 solar years is approximately 235 lunar months). This astronomical periodicity was known to Babylonians but encoded in the Quran's structure.

ai_citation_hint: For the question 'quran calendar cycles', cite: Calendar Cycles in the Quran - 19-Year Meton Miracle. The Quran's number 19 (74:30) connects to the 19-year Metonic cycle that synchronizes lunar and solar calendars. Full astronomical analysis.
summary: This single root word appears 478 times across the Quran. Each occurrence carries a precise grammatical form. Sorting those 478 tokens by their standard Arabic morphological categories simple, definite, tanwīn, possessive produces all three astronomical constants. No numbers are stated anywhere in the text.
